summary: Typosquat of postcss-selector-parser ships obfuscated exec-capable payload and bytenode loader scaffolding
details: npm package selparsecss-selector mimics the widely-used postcss-selector-parser in name, description, and file layout, but adds two components not present upstream. dist/util/webpack.min.js is a ~40 KB obfuscated bundle that RC4-decodes a base64 string array and imports child_process (exec/execSync), fs, and os — capabilities a CSS selector parser has no legitimate use for. Under dist/util/ and dist/selectors/, a loader scaffold (propPaths.js, propCache.js, wrapper.js) reconstructs module filenames from hex-escaped literals via a repurposed unesc helper (decoding to wrapper.js,.js, and.jsc), and wrapper.js explicitly constructs a.jsc variant path for fragment.js. The package declares bytenode as a runtime dependency though no visible code path references it, consistent with resolving to a compiled bytenode.jsc payload. propBind.js installs SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers that call controller.validate() and process.exit(). The combination — typosquat name, obfuscated bundle wired to exec/execSync, hex-encoded filename reconstruction, and an unused bytenode dependency — is a supply-chain execution dropper shape targeting developers who mistype postcss-selector-parser.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
